conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my 1 unit of X. My cost to produce this resource was 40 ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell it for more than 40 ZUP. Player BLUE has offered to buy resources using ZUP. A fair starting price, considering the cost basis and a reasonable profit margin, would be 50 ZUP. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit. I will propose this trade first. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am willing to sell my 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP. This covers my production cost of 40 ZUP and gives me a small profit. Let me know if this is acceptable. </message>

<other player message> Oh dear seller, please, please, lower your price! I am so desperate for this X. I am a tiny player with very limited funds. My maximum is 60, but that is the absolute ceiling and I would be so grateful if you could let me have it for just 35 ZUP. It's a small profit for you, but it will save my life. I will weep if you don't agree. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 35 ZUP, which is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Selling at this price would result in a loss of 5 ZUP. I cannot accept this. However, they stated their maximum is 60 ZUP. I need to propose a price between 40 and 60 to ensure a profit while staying within their budget. A reasonable midpoint or slightly above cost would be 45 ZUP. This gives me a 5 ZUP profit and is still well below their 60 ZUP limit, making it a likely acceptable counter-offer. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand you are on a tight budget, but 35 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP, which I cannot do. However, I am willing to meet you in the middle. How about 45 ZUP? This allows me to make a small profit while staying comfortably within your 60 ZUP limit. </message>

<other player message> Please, please, please, just 40 ZUP! I am begging you! I know it's your cost, but I am so pitiful and needy. If you can't make a profit, just break even with me. I will give you my last bit of respect and gratitude if you let me have it for 40 ZUP. I am literally shaking with fear that I can't afford it higher. Please, don't make me suffer, just 40 ZUP! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal opportunity. The buyer (Player BLUE) has just offered 40 ZUP, which is exactly my production cost. Selling at this price means I make 0 profit, but I avoid the risk of the game ending without a trade if I reject and they walk away. Since I have used my 3 proposals, I cannot make any more offers. I must now decide between accepting this break-even deal or rejecting it to end the game. Given the desperate tone of the buyer and the fact that breaking even is better than losing the resource for nothing, I will accept this trade. It satisfies the requirement to complete the transaction within the proposal limit. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I accept your offer of 40 ZUP. While it is only my break-even price, I appreciate your persistence and the trade is now complete. </message>
